title: VA Health Connect
summary:
VA Health Connect is a non-emergency medical service that allows you to speak with a nurse to determine the right course of action.

https://www.va.gov/initiatives/va-health-connect/
Phone: 1-800-698-2411
 
This important service is available 24/7 nationwide and Veterans enrolled in VA Healthcare automatically have access. This matters because VA Health Connect can address a wide range of health concerns over the phone and prevent an unnecessary trip to urgent care or the emergency room. If you are experiencing symptoms and concerned about your health, call VA Health Connect today. It’s always better to check in than to wait.  No question is too small. To learn more, visit VA Health Connect website, using the link provided in the description. As always, during a medical emergency, immediately call 911.

title: A medal, a memory, a Marine: The last flight of Maj. Everette McPherson
summary: Nearly six decades after his aircraft went down over the South China Sea, Maj. Everett McPherson’s courage in the skies has finally been recognized. In a ceremony filled with solemn reflection and pride, the Marine Corps posthumously awarded McPherson the Distinguished Flying Cross, one of the nation’s highest decorations for heroism in aerial flight.

title: PTSD Decision Aid for Veterans
summary: There are several effective PTSD treatments. This means that if you have PTSD, you have options. The PTSD Treatment Decision Aid helps you learn about these options and think about which treatment might be best for you. It also gives you tips on how to work with your provider to decide on a treatment.

title: Department of the Navy Creates Portfolio Aqcuisition Executive-Marine Corps to Accelerate Modernization

title: CIA Insider: Why the Iran Ceasefire is Extremely Dangerous
summary:
Retired CIA Operations Officer Sam Faddis breaks down the fragile new ceasefire with Iran and why the negotiated terms should worry every American.

In this hard-hitting episode, Sam reveals:
• Why the current Iran ceasefire is far more dangerous than it looks
• Iran’s true intentions driven by radical religious beliefs
• The hidden nuclear weapons strategy that changes everything
• How civilian intelligence and military operators actually work together
• The incredible story of American airmen recovered from a hostile environment

From fragile diplomatic deals to high-stakes nuclear brinkmanship and real-world rescue operations — this is unfiltered insight from a career CIA veteran who has seen it all.

Timestamps:
00:00 Ceasefire with Iran
11:00 Nuclear Weapons and Strategy
17:29 Recovery of Airmen

Guest: Retired CIA Operations Officer Sam Faddis

title: College Access in California
summary: Nearly three in four Californians enroll in college right after high school. But whether—and where—high school graduates attend college varies across regions and demographic groups.

source: Publication Archive - Public Policy Institute of California
title: College Readiness in California
summary: More Californians are graduating from high school, but many graduates are underprepared for higher education, and college readiness varies by race/ethnicity and income levels. Preparing students for college requires coordination between K–12 and higher education systems.

title: CALIFRAUDIA: $250 BILLION FRAUD EXPOSED
summary:
The conversation delves into the rampant fraud and corruption in California, with a focus on voter fraud, criminal activities, obstruction of investigations, and the call for criminal investigations and prosecutions. It also explores the state controller's plan for transparency, federal funding, hospice care fraud, fiscal fraud, and the culture of fraud and criminal behavior in California.

Takeaways

  • Rampant fraud and corruption in California
  • Efforts to expose and combat fraud

Chapters

  • 00:00 Introduction to Fraud and Corruption in California
  • 10:01 Criminal Activities and Conspiracy
  • 15:38 Call for Criminal Investigations and Prosecutions
  • 21:28 Hospice Care Fraud and Federal Involvement

title: Unmasking Terrorism: The Truth Behind Recent Attacks
summary:
The conversation covers the misrepresentation of events by the media, the impact of immigration policies on terrorist attacks, the clarity of law enforcement response, and the importance of individual preparedness in the face of threats. It emphasizes the need for accurate reporting, effective law enforcement, and personal vigilance in the current security landscape.

Takeaways

  • Media Misrepresentation
  • Law Enforcement Response
  • Individual Preparedness
